---
title: "Local refinement scan | J = 48-60 | factor_corr = 0.6 | N = 500-1000"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_depth: 2
date: "2026-05-06"
---

# Purpose

This file runs a narrower refinement scan for `factor_corr = 0.6`, with `J = 48-60`.  
The N range is restricted to 500-1000 to zoom in on the crossing region identified in the broader scan.  
It compares lslx and CC-ESEM across this range and saves the summary results.


```{r setup, include=FALSE}

# Load the required helper scripts. Replace the example paths with your own local paths.
source("C:/Users/YourName/Desktop/BEP/R/rESEM-CC.R")
source("C:/Users/YourName/Desktop/BEP/R/generation_function.R")

# Load required packages
library(MASS); library(lavaan); library(lslx)
```

```{r helper_functions}
# ------------------------------------------------------------
# build_lslx_model()
#   Constructs the lslx model string for a J-item, n_factors-factor EFA.
#   Each factor has J/n_factors "primary" items (free loadings, :=>)
#   and the remaining items as "cross-loadings" (penalised, :~>).
#   Factor variances are fixed to 1.
#   Note: factor covariances are NOT constrained here, allowing
#   oblique factors consistent with factor_corr = 0.6.
# ------------------------------------------------------------
build_lslx_model <- function(J, n_factors = 3) {
  J_per_factor <- J / n_factors          # number of items per factor
  lines <- c()

  # Primary (free) loadings: factor k loads freely on its own items
  for (k in 1:n_factors) {
    main_items <- paste0("x", ((k-1)*J_per_factor + 1):(k*J_per_factor))
    lines <- c(lines, paste0("f", k, " :=> ", paste(main_items, collapse = " + ")))
  }

  # Cross-loadings (penalised): factor k is allowed to load on items from other factors
  for (k in 1:n_factors) {
    other_items <- paste0("x", setdiff(1:J, ((k-1)*J_per_factor + 1):(k*J_per_factor)))
    lines <- c(lines, paste0("f", k, " :~> ", paste(other_items, collapse = " + ")))
  }

  # Fix each factor's variance to 1 (standardise factors)
  for (k in 1:n_factors)
    lines <- c(lines, paste0("f", k, " <=> fix(1) * f", k))

  paste(lines, collapse = "\n")
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# align_loadings()
#   Matches estimated factors to the true factors by maximum
#   absolute correlation (greedy assignment), then flips sign
#   where needed so that each estimated column correlates
#   positively with its matched true column.
#   Returns the re-ordered and sign-corrected loading matrix.
# ------------------------------------------------------------
align_loadings <- function(Lambda_est, Lambda_true) {
  n_factors <- ncol(Lambda_true)

  # Build an n_factors x n_factors matrix of absolute correlations
  # between every pair of (estimated factor, true factor) columns
  cor_mat <- matrix(NA, n_factors, n_factors)
  for (i in 1:n_factors)
    for (j in 1:n_factors)
      cor_mat[i, j] <- abs(cor(Lambda_est[, i], Lambda_true[, j]))

  # Greedy matching: for each true factor j, pick the estimated factor
  # with the highest absolute correlation (that has not been used yet)
  order_est <- rep(NA, n_factors); used <- rep(FALSE, n_factors)
  for (j in 1:n_factors) {
    best_i <- which.max(cor_mat[, j] * (!used))
    order_est[j] <- best_i; used[best_i] <- TRUE
  }

  # Re-order estimated loading columns to match true factor order
  Lambda_aligned <- Lambda_est[, order_est]

  # Flip sign of each column if it correlates negatively with the true column
  for (j in 1:n_factors)
    if (cor(Lambda_aligned[, j], Lambda_true[, j]) < 0)
      Lambda_aligned[, j] <- -Lambda_aligned[, j]

  colnames(Lambda_aligned) <- paste0("f", 1:n_factors)
  return(Lambda_aligned)
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# run_lslx()
#   factor_corr = 0.6 version: data is z-scored before fitting,
#   and lambda_grid is fixed to seq(0, 0.1, 0.05).
#   Fits a LASSO-penalised SEM via lslx::plsem() on one dataset.
#   Steps:
#     1. z-score the data
#     2. build the model string
#     3. fit with a small lambda grid; select lambda by BIC
#     4. extract the loading matrix ("y<-f" block)
#     5. optionally align to the true loadings
#   Returns a list: converged (TRUE/FALSE) and loadings (matrix or NULL).
# ------------------------------------------------------------
run_lslx <- function(X, J, n_factors = 3, Lambda_true = NULL) {
  # Standardise data (lslx expects this for interpretable penalties)
  X_df <- as.data.frame(scale(as.matrix(X)))
  colnames(X_df) <- paste0("x", 1:J)
  model <- build_lslx_model(J = J, n_factors = n_factors)
  result <- tryCatch({
    fit <- plsem(model = model, data = X_df,
                 penalty_method = "lasso", lambda_grid = seq(0.00, 0.10, 0.05))

    # Extract loading matrix selected by BIC
    lm <- fit$extract_coefficient_matrix(selector = "bic", block = "y<-f")$g
    rownames(lm) <- paste0("x", 1:J)
    colnames(lm) <- paste0("f", 1:n_factors)
    if (!is.null(Lambda_true)) lm <- align_loadings(lm, Lambda_true)
    list(converged = TRUE, loadings = lm)
  }, error = function(e) list(converged = FALSE, loadings = NULL))
  return(result)
}

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# run_approxESEM()
#   Fits the CC-regularized ESEM (cardinality-constrained)
#   via MULTISTART_CCrESEM() with multiple random starts.
#   Steps:
#     1. set default cardinality = J/n_factors per factor
#     2. z-score the data
#     3. run multi-start optimisation
#     4. optionally align to the true loadings
#   Returns a list: converged and loadings.
# ------------------------------------------------------------
run_approxESEM <- function(X, J, n_factors = 3, card = NULL,
                           nstarts = 20, Lambda_true = NULL) {
  # Default: each factor is allowed to load on J/n_factors items
  if (is.null(card)) card <- rep(J / n_factors, n_factors)
  X_scaled <- scale(as.matrix(X))
  result <- tryCatch({
    fit <- MULTISTART_CCrESEM(DATA = X_scaled, R = n_factors, CARD = card,
                              MaxIter = 1000, eps = 1e-6, nstarts = nstarts)
    lm <- fit$loadings
    rownames(lm) <- paste0("x", 1:J)
    colnames(lm) <- paste0("f", 1:n_factors)
    if (!is.null(Lambda_true)) lm <- align_loadings(lm, Lambda_true)
    list(converged = fit$converged, loadings = lm)
  }, error = function(e) list(converged = FALSE, loadings = NULL))
  return(result)
}
```

```{r main_scan}

# Main simulation scan
#   Narrowed N range (500-1000) compared to the broader J48-60 scan,
#   targeting the crossing region identified in the initial pass.
#   For each combination of (J, N) we run n_rep = 50 replications,
#   fit both lslx and CC-ESEM, and collect:
#     - MSE  (mean squared error of loading estimates vs. true loadings)
#     - AB   (average absolute bias)
#     - Var  (average variance of loading estimates across replications)
#     - Runtime (elapsed seconds per fit)
#     - Convergence rate for lslx

set.seed(42)
n_rep <- 50    # number of Monte Carlo replications per (J, N) cell

# N grids to scan for each value of J (uniform 500-1000, step 100)
scan_grid <- list(
  "48" = seq(500, 1000, by = 100),
  "51" = seq(500, 1000, by = 100),
  "54" = seq(500, 1000, by = 100),
  "57" = seq(500, 1000, by = 100),
  "60" = seq(500, 1000, by = 100)
)

J_seq_main <- c(48, 51, 54, 57, 60)
results_all <- list()   # will accumulate one data.frame row per (J, N) cell

cat("=== Full scan: J = 48-60, factor_corr = 0.6, N = 500-1000 ===\n\n")

for (J in J_seq_main) {
  N_vec <- scan_grid[[as.character(J)]]
  cat(">> J =", J, "| N:", min(N_vec), "~", max(N_vec),
      "| step: 100 |", length(N_vec), "N values x 50 reps\n")

  for (N in N_vec) {
    # Pre-allocate storage for this (J, N) cell
    lslx_lm_list  <- vector("list", n_rep)   # loading matrices from lslx
    esem_lm_list  <- vector("list", n_rep)   # loading matrices from CC-ESEM
    lslx_conv     <- logical(n_rep)          # convergence flag for lslx
    lslx_time_vec <- numeric(n_rep)          # elapsed time for lslx
    esem_time_vec <- numeric(n_rep)          # elapsed time for CC-ESEM

    for (rep in 1:n_rep) {
      # Generate one dataset from the true factor model
      # PL = primary loading size; reliability = 0.36; factor_corr = 0.6 (oblique)
      dgp <- data_generation(
        PL = 0.6, nvar.factor = J / 3, nfactors = 3,
        reliability = 0.36, factor_corr = 0.6,
        N = N, beta = c(0.5, 0.3, 0.2), VAFy = 0.5, CL = FALSE
      )

      # Fit both methods and record elapsed time
      t1 <- system.time({ r1 <- run_lslx(X = dgp$X, J = J, Lambda_true = dgp$Ptrue) })
      t2 <- system.time({ r2 <- run_approxESEM(X = dgp$X, J = J, Lambda_true = dgp$Ptrue) })

      lslx_conv[rep]      <- r1$converged
      lslx_lm_list[[rep]] <- if (r1$converged) r1$loadings else NULL
      esem_lm_list[[rep]] <- if (r2$converged) r2$loadings else NULL
      lslx_time_vec[rep]  <- t1["elapsed"]
      esem_time_vec[rep]  <- t2["elapsed"]
    }

    Lambda_true <- dgp$Ptrue   # true loading matrix (same across reps for this cell)

    # Keep only successful (non-NULL) replications
    lslx_valid <- Filter(Negate(is.null), lslx_lm_list)
    esem_valid <- Filter(Negate(is.null), esem_lm_list)

    # --- Compute summary statistics for lslx ---
    if (length(lslx_valid) > 1) {
      lslx_arr     <- simplify2array(lslx_valid)          # J x n_factors x n_valid array
      lslx_mean_lm <- apply(lslx_arr, c(1, 2), mean)     # element-wise mean over reps

      # MSE: for each rep compute mean squared error, then average across reps
      lslx_mse_vec <- sapply(lslx_valid, function(lm) mean((lm - Lambda_true)^2))
      lslx_mse     <- mean(lslx_mse_vec); lslx_mse_sd <- sd(lslx_mse_vec)

      # Average absolute bias: |mean estimate - true value|, averaged over all elements
      lslx_ab  <- mean(abs(lslx_mean_lm - Lambda_true))

      # Average variance of each loading across replications
      lslx_var <- mean(apply(lslx_arr, c(1, 2), var))
    } else { lslx_mse <- NA; lslx_mse_sd <- NA; lslx_ab <- NA; lslx_var <- NA }

    # --- Compute summary statistics for CC-ESEM ---
    if (length(esem_valid) > 1) {
      esem_arr     <- simplify2array(esem_valid)
      esem_mean_lm <- apply(esem_arr, c(1, 2), mean)

      esem_mse_vec <- sapply(esem_valid, function(lm) mean((lm - Lambda_true)^2))
      esem_mse     <- mean(esem_mse_vec); esem_mse_sd <- sd(esem_mse_vec)
      esem_ab      <- mean(abs(esem_mean_lm - Lambda_true))
      esem_var     <- mean(apply(esem_arr, c(1, 2), var))
    } else { esem_mse <- NA; esem_mse_sd <- NA; esem_ab <- NA; esem_var <- NA }

    # Store one row of results for this (J, N) combination
    results_all[[paste0("J", J, "_N", N)]] <- data.frame(
      J = J, N = N, JN_ratio = round(J / N, 5),    # item-to-sample ratio
      lslx_mse_mean = lslx_mse, lslx_mse_sd = lslx_mse_sd,
      esem_mse_mean = esem_mse, esem_mse_sd = esem_mse_sd,
      mse_diff  = esem_mse - lslx_mse,              # positive = ESEM worse
      lslx_ab   = lslx_ab,  esem_ab  = esem_ab,  ab_diff  = esem_ab  - lslx_ab,
      lslx_var  = lslx_var, esem_var = esem_var,  var_diff = esem_var - lslx_var,
      lslx_time = mean(lslx_time_vec), esem_time = mean(esem_time_vec),
      lslx_conv_rate = mean(lslx_conv)               # proportion of reps that converged
    )
  }
  cat("   Done: J =", J, "\n\n")
}

# Combine all rows into a single data frame, sorted by J then N
tp_df <- do.call(rbind, results_all)
tp_df <- tp_df[order(tp_df$J, tp_df$N), ]
rownames(tp_df) <- NULL
cat("=== Scan complete:", nrow(tp_df), "rows ===\n")
```

```{r print_tables}

# Print summary tables and automatically detect MSE crossings
#   A "crossing" is a J/N ratio where lslx and CC-ESEM swap
#   which method has lower MSE (mse_diff changes sign).

crossing_summary <- list()

cat("\n====== MSE difference table & crossing intervals ======\n")

for (J in J_seq_main) {
  sub   <- tp_df[tp_df$J == J, ]; sub <- sub[order(sub$N), ]

  # Find indices where the sign of mse_diff flips (crossing point)
  cross <- which(diff(sign(sub$mse_diff)) != 0)

  cat("\n--- J =", J, "---\n")
  print(sub[, c("N", "JN_ratio", "lslx_mse_mean", "esem_mse_mean",
                "mse_diff", "lslx_ab", "esem_ab", "lslx_var", "esem_var",
                "lslx_conv_rate")], digits = 4, row.names = FALSE)

  if (length(cross) > 0) {
    N_lo <- sub$N[cross]; N_hi <- sub$N[cross+1]
    JN_lo <- sub$JN_ratio[cross+1]; JN_hi <- sub$JN_ratio[cross]
    JN_mid <- round(mean(c(JN_lo, JN_hi)), 5)
    cat("  * MSE Crossing: N in [", N_lo, ",", N_hi, "]",
        "| J/N in [", round(JN_lo,4), ",", round(JN_hi,4), "]",
        "| midpoint J/N ~", JN_mid, "\n")
    crossing_summary[[as.character(J)]] <- data.frame(
      J=J, N_lo=N_lo, N_hi=N_hi, JN_lo=JN_lo, JN_hi=JN_hi, JN_mid=JN_mid)
  } else {
    cat("  [!] No MSE crossing detected in the current N range\n")
    crossing_summary[[as.character(J)]] <- data.frame(
      J=J, N_lo=NA, N_hi=NA, JN_lo=NA, JN_hi=NA, JN_mid=NA)
  }
}

# Summarise all crossing points in a single table
crossing_df <- do.call(rbind, crossing_summary)
rownames(crossing_df) <- NULL
cat("\n====== MSE Crossing Summary ======\n")
print(crossing_df, row.names=FALSE, digits=5)
```

```{r plot_mse_diff}

# Plot: MSE difference (ESEM - lslx) vs N
#   One panel per J value.
#   The horizontal dashed line at 0 marks the break-even point.
#   Vertical dotted lines bracket the crossing interval when detected.

par(mfrow = c(2, 3), mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1), oma = c(0, 0, 2.5, 0))
for (J in J_seq_main) {
  sub   <- tp_df[tp_df$J == J, ]; sub <- sub[order(sub$N), ]
  cross <- which(diff(sign(sub$mse_diff)) != 0)
  y <- sub$mse_diff; ylim <- range(y, na.rm=TRUE); ylim <- ylim + c(-0.05,0.20)*diff(ylim)
  plot(sub$N, y, type="b", pch=16, col="#2A9D8F", lwd=2, lty=2,
       xlab="N", ylab="MSE diff (ESEM - lslx)", main=paste0("J = ",J), ylim=ylim)
  abline(h=0, lty=2, col="gray50", lwd=1.2)   # break-even line
  if (length(cross) > 0) {
    # Mark the two N values that bracket the crossing
    abline(v=sub$N[cross], lty=3, col="gray40")
    abline(v=sub$N[cross+1], lty=3, col="gray40")
    # Annotate with the approximate J/N ratio at the crossing
    text(mean(c(sub$N[cross],sub$N[cross+1])), max(y,na.rm=TRUE),
         paste0("J/N ~ ",round(mean(c(sub$JN_ratio[cross],sub$JN_ratio[cross+1])),4)),
         adj=c(0.5,-0.3), cex=0.85, col="gray20")
  }
}
plot.new()   # empty 6th panel to keep layout symmetric
mtext("MSE Diff (ESEM - lslx) vs N  |  factor_corr = 0.6  |  N = 500-1000  |  50 reps",
      outer=TRUE, cex=1.1, font=2)
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
```

```{r plot_mse_abs}
# ============================================================
# Plot: Absolute MSE vs N for both methods (with +/-1 SD band)
#   Red  = lslx
#   Blue = CC-ESEM
#   Shaded bands show +/- 1 SD across the 50 replications.
# ============================================================
par(mfrow = c(2, 3), mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1), oma = c(0, 0, 2.5, 0))
for (J in J_seq_main) {
  sub  <- tp_df[tp_df$J == J, ]; sub <- sub[order(sub$N), ]

  # y-axis range covering both methods' mean +/- SD
  ylim <- range(c(sub$lslx_mse_mean-sub$lslx_mse_sd, sub$esem_mse_mean-sub$esem_mse_sd,
                  sub$lslx_mse_mean+sub$lslx_mse_sd, sub$esem_mse_mean+sub$esem_mse_sd), na.rm=TRUE)

  # lslx: mean line + SD band
  plot(sub$N, sub$lslx_mse_mean, type="b", pch=16, col="#E63946", lwd=2,
       ylim=ylim, xlab="N", ylab="MSE", main=paste0("J = ",J))
  polygon(c(sub$N,rev(sub$N)),
          c(sub$lslx_mse_mean+sub$lslx_mse_sd, rev(sub$lslx_mse_mean-sub$lslx_mse_sd)),
          col=adjustcolor("#E63946",0.15), border=NA)

  # CC-ESEM: mean line + SD band
  lines(sub$N, sub$esem_mse_mean, type="b", pch=17, col="#457B9D", lwd=2, lty=2)
  polygon(c(sub$N,rev(sub$N)),
          c(sub$esem_mse_mean+sub$esem_mse_sd, rev(sub$esem_mse_mean-sub$esem_mse_sd)),
          col=adjustcolor("#457B9D",0.15), border=NA)
  legend("topright", legend=c("lslx","CC-ESEM"), col=c("#E63946","#457B9D"),
         lwd=2, pch=c(16,17), lty=c(1,2), bty="n", cex=0.8)
}
plot.new()
mtext("MSE vs N  |  lslx vs CC-ESEM  |  factor_corr = 0.6  |  N = 500-1000  |  50 reps",
      outer=TRUE, cex=1.1, font=2)
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
```

```{r plot_ab}
# ============================================================
# Figure A: Average Absolute Bias vs N
#   Bias = |mean estimated loading - true loading|, averaged over
#   all J x n_factors loading elements.
#   Lower is better; shows whether estimates are systematically
#   shifted away from truth.
# ============================================================
par(mfrow = c(2, 3), mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1), oma = c(0, 0, 2.5, 0))
for (J in J_seq_main) {
  sub  <- tp_df[tp_df$J == J, ]; sub <- sub[order(sub$N), ]
  ylim <- range(c(sub$lslx_ab, sub$esem_ab), na.rm=TRUE)
  ylim <- ylim + c(-0.02, 0.08)*diff(ylim)
  plot(sub$N, sub$lslx_ab, type="b", pch=16, col="#E63946", lwd=2,
       ylim=ylim, xlab="N", ylab="Average Absolute Bias", main=paste0("J = ",J))
  lines(sub$N, sub$esem_ab, type="b", pch=17, col="#457B9D", lwd=2, lty=2)
  legend("topright", legend=c("lslx","CC-ESEM"), col=c("#E63946","#457B9D"),
         lwd=2, pch=c(16,17), lty=c(1,2), bty="n", cex=0.8)
}
plot.new()
mtext("Figure A: Average Absolute Bias vs N  |  factor_corr = 0.6  |  N = 500-1000  |  50 reps",
      outer=TRUE, cex=1.1, font=2)
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
```

```{r plot_var}
# ============================================================
# Figure B: Variance of Loading Estimates vs N
#   Variance here = average element-wise variance of the loading
#   matrix across replications.
#   Lower is better; shows estimation stability as N grows.
# ============================================================
par(mfrow = c(2, 3), mar = c(4, 4, 3, 1), oma = c(0, 0, 2.5, 0))
for (J in J_seq_main) {
  sub  <- tp_df[tp_df$J == J, ]; sub <- sub[order(sub$N), ]
  ylim <- range(c(sub$lslx_var, sub$esem_var), na.rm=TRUE)
  ylim <- ylim + c(-0.02, 0.08)*diff(ylim)
  plot(sub$N, sub$lslx_var, type="b", pch=16, col="#E63946", lwd=2,
       ylim=ylim, xlab="N", ylab="Average Variance of Loading Estimates",
       main=paste0("J = ",J))
  lines(sub$N, sub$esem_var, type="b", pch=17, col="#457B9D", lwd=2, lty=2)
  legend("topright", legend=c("lslx","CC-ESEM"), col=c("#E63946","#457B9D"),
         lwd=2, pch=c(16,17), lty=c(1,2), bty="n", cex=0.8)
}
plot.new()
mtext("Figure B: Variance of Loading Estimates vs N  |  factor_corr = 0.6  |  N = 500-1000  |  50 reps",
      outer=TRUE, cex=1.1, font=2)
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
```
